items.title: Mexican Mayor Shot Dead in his Office After Surviving Earlier Hit
items.description: Armed assailants gunned down the mayor of a small Mexican city just south of the country’s capital in his office in the latest case of suspected gangland violence targeting a local official.

items.title: Yen’s Slump in Trade-Weighted Gauge Shows Its Broad-Based Drop
items.description: While the yen’s plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the currency’s strength is sending an equally worrying signal for Japan.

items.title: Ebola Deaths Top 1,000 in Congo as Response Trails Fastest-Spreading Outbreak
items.description: Confirmed Ebola deaths in Democratic Republic of Congo have surpassed 1,000, prompting Africa’s top public health official to warn the outbreak could become one of the worst on record unless transmiss

items.title: China’s High-Profile Funds Ditch Consumer Stocks to Chase AI
items.description: Some high-profile Chinese funds heavily invested in consumer names are pivoting to AI stocks, a bet that may boost returns but also leaves them more vulnerable to the sector’s volatility.

items.title: Nasdaq to Kick Off Tiny Companies Faster After SEC Approves Rule
items.description: Nasdaq Inc. will change its rules to make it easier to kick off flailing businesses as regulators zero in on wild market swings and allegations of manipulation that have plagued trading in so-called m

items.title: Gold Holds Gain as Dip-Buying Counters Inflation Fears From War
items.description: Gold held gains on dip-buying even as hostilities in the Middle East pushed oil prices to multiweek highs, rekindling concerns that US inflationary pressure could prompt the Federal Reserve to raise i

items.title: Hong Kong’s Offshore Yuan Market Evolves Into Global Funding Hub
items.description: A record pile of yuan in Hong Kong is making its way to corporate loans while driving a surge in capital market activity, signaling that China’s drive to internationalize its currency is advancing.

items.title: Asian Shares Rise as Chipmakers Gain, Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian shares advanced as regional chipmakers gained on expectations they will benefit from billions of dollars flowing into the artificial intelligence buildout. Oil extended its recent gains.

items.title: Wall Street Banks Trading Parts of $35 Billion AI Chip Deal
items.description: Wall Street banks have started buying and selling an initial piece of a $35 billion financing package for Broadcom Inc. and Anthropic PBC’s AI infrastructure expansion, with the biggest private credit

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
items.description: Oil extended gains after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.

items.title: Brent Crude Spikes in Late Trading After Red Sea Tanker Attack
items.description: Oil prices rose sharply in post-settlement trading Wednesday, with Brent crude oil rising near $95 a barrel after Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels said they struck two Saudi tankers.

items.title: Vitamin Well Raises $2.2 Billion From Cross-Currency Loan Deal
items.description: Vitamin Well AB raised about $2.2 billion through a cross-currency loan offering to fund its acquisition of protein-bar maker EMPWR Nutrition Group.

items.title: Daiwa AM Boosts Staff in Shift Toward Institutional Investors
items.description: Daiwa Asset Management Co. is planning to double its sales staff targeting institutional investors to about 20 people, its president said.

items.title: Mexico City’s Casa de Toño Restaurant Chain Explores Sale, Eyes Growth
items.description: Mexican family-owned restaurant chain La Casa de Toño is seeking a sale in a deal that could top $400 million and set the business up for expansion, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Southwest Hedges Full-Year Guidance as Fuel Expenses Tick Up
items.description: Southwest Airlines Co. broadened its guidance for full-year 2026 to account for uncertainty resulting from the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran, which sent fuel expenses higher in the second q

items.title: Wall Street Won’t Give Up on Strategy, Sees 170% Upside
items.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. is confronting a collapse of confidence almost everywhere — except among Wall Street analysts.

items.title: GE Vernova Falls as Outlook Clashes With ‘Too High’ AI Hopes
items.description: GE Vernova Inc.’s shares slumped after a higher revenue outlook failed to impress investors, a sign that it’s getting harder for companies that sell equipment to data centers to meet the market’s sky-

items.title: JPY/USD: Why Is Japan Yen So Weak? Can Government Intervention Help Currency?
items.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.

items.title: Yen Rebound Ebbs After Brief Rally on Outlook for BOJ Rate Hikes
items.description: The yen slid beyond 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground in choppy trading on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

items.title: Stocks Churn Late as Big-Tech Earnings Kick Off: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street traders sent stocks wavering in late hours as they sifted through results from megacaps for clues on whether their massive artificial-intelligence investments are paying off.
